fact: August 23 is exactly 235 days into the year, which means there are exactly 130 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when many spiders start spinning their biggest, most impressive webs because they're getting ready to catch extra food before winter.
The sun rises about 2 minutes later than it did just a week ago, and you can actually notice the difference if you pay attention!
This date falls right in the middle of Virgo season, making it one of the most 'Virgo-ish' days of the entire year.
In many places, August 23 is when morning dew starts getting really heavy and thick on grass, making everything look like it's covered in tiny diamonds.
Cicadas are typically screaming their loudest around this time because they know summer is running out and they need to find mates fast!
The Earth is tilted at exactly the same angle it was on February 20th, but we're on the opposite side of the sun, so everything feels completely different.
In the Southern Hemisphere, this is when ski resorts often hit their peak snow conditions because winter is at its strongest.
Many fruit trees are so heavy with ripe fruit right now that their branches actually bend down and touch the ground.
Squirrels often go extra busy around this date because their instincts tell them to start hoarding food, even though winter is still months away.
This is when many flowers start producing their stickiest, smelliest nectar because they're working hard to attract the last pollinators of summer.
